title: Two Sum
description:
Find indices of two numbers that add up to target.

**Example**
```
nums = [2,7,11,15], target = 9
Output: [0,1]
```

**Hash Map (O(n))**
Store each value's index. For each element, check if complement exists.

title: Sliding Window Max Sum
description:
Maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of size `k`.

**Example**
```
Input:  [2,1,5,1,3,2], k=3
Output: 9 (subarray [5,1,3])
```

**O(n)** time, **O(1)** space.

title: Binary Search
description:
Find target in sorted array. Return index or -1.

**Example**
```
Input:  [1,3,5,7,9], target=5
Output: 2
```

**O(log n)** time, **O(1)** space.

title: Best Time to Buy & Sell
description:
Buy low, sell high with one transaction.

**Example**
```
Input:  [7,1,5,3,6,4]
Output: 5 (buy at 1, sell at 6)
```

**O(n)** time, **O(1)** space. Return 0 if no profit.

title: Product of Array Except Self
description:
Return array where output[i] = product of all elements except nums[i]. **No division**. **O(n)**.

**Example**
```
Input:  [1,2,3,4]
Output: [24,12,8,6]
```

Two passes: left prefix products then right suffix products.

title: Valid Palindrome
description:
Check if string is palindrome (alphanumeric only, ignore case).

**Example**
```
Input:  "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"
Output: true ("amanaplanacanalpanama")
```

Two pointers from both ends, skip non-alphanumeric.

testCases.input: ["A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"]

title: Valid Anagram
description:
Check if t is anagram of s.

**Example**
```
Input:  s="anagram", t="nagaram"
Output: true
```

Count char frequencies. Both strings must match.

title: Longest Substring (No Repeats)
description:
Find length of longest substring without repeating chars.

**Example**
```
Input:  "abcabcbb"
Output: 3 ("abc")
```

Sliding window with Set. **O(n)** time.

title: First Unique Character
description:
Find first non-repeating character in string. Return index or -1.

**Example**
```
Input:  "leetcode"
Output: 0 ("l" is first unique)
```

Two passes: count frequencies, find first with count=1.

title: Reverse Words in String
description:
Reverse order of words. Handle multiple spaces.

**Example**
```
Input:  "the sky is blue"
Output: "blue is sky the"
```

Single space between words in output, no leading/trailing spaces.
starterCode:
const [s] = input;
return s.trim().split(/\s+/).reverse().join(' ');

title: Bubble Sort
description:
Sort array using adjacent swaps. Each pass bubbles largest element to end. Optimized: stop if no swap occurred.

**O(n²)** worst, **O(n)** best.

title: Merge Sort
description:
Divide array into halves, recursively sort each, merge sorted halves.

**O(n log n)** time, **O(n)** space.

title: Quick Sort
description:
Pick pivot, partition array around it, recursively sort partitions.

**O(n log n)** average, **O(n²)** worst. Uses Lomuto partition.

title: Insertion Sort
description:
Build sorted array one element at a time. Insert each element into its correct position among already-sorted elements.

**O(n²)** worst, **O(n)** best (nearly sorted).

title: Contains Duplicate
description:
Return true if any value appears at least twice.

**Example**
```
Input:  [1,2,3,1]
Output: true
```

Use Set. **O(n)** time, **O(n)** space.

title: Intersection of Two Arrays
description:
Return unique elements common to both arrays.

**Example**
```
Input:  nums1=[1,2,2,1], nums2=[2,2]
Output: [2]
```

Use Set for O(1) lookups. Result sorted.

title: Maximum Subarray (Kadane)
description:
Find contiguous subarray with largest sum (Kadane's algorithm).

**Example**
```
Input:  [-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1,-5,4]
Output: 6
```

**O(n)** time, **O(1)** space.

title: Climbing Stairs
description:
Count distinct ways to climb n steps (1 or 2 steps at a time).

**Example**
```
Input:  n=3
Output: 3
```
Ways: (1,1,1), (1,2), (2,1)

**O(n)** time, **O(1)** space. Fibonacci sequence.

title: House Robber
description:
Max money from robbing non-adjacent houses.

**Example**
```
Input:  [1,2,3,1]
Output: 4 (rob 1+3: 1+3=4)
```

**O(n)** time, **O(1)** space. DP with two vars.

title: Reverse Linked List
description:
Reverse a singly linked list. Input/output as arrays (converted to ListNode internally).

**Example**
```
Input:  [1,2,3,4,5]
Output: [5,4,3,2,1]
```

Use prev/curr/next pointers. **O(n)** time, **O(1)** space.

title: Linked List Cycle Detection
description:
Detect if linked list has a cycle. Second value in input = cycle position (0-indexed) or -1.

**Example**
```
Input:  [1,2,3,4], pos=0 (cycle at index 0)
Output: true
```

Floyd's Tortoise & Hare. **O(n)** time, **O(1)** space.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description:
Merge two sorted linked lists into one sorted list.

**Example**
```
Input:  [1,2,4], [1,3,4]
Output: [1,1,2,3,4,4]
```

**O(n+m)** time, **O(1)** space.

title: Max Depth of Binary Tree
description:
Find maximum depth (height) of binary tree. Input is level-order array (null = no child).

**Example**
```
Input:  [3,9,20,null,null,15,7]
Output: 3
```

Recursive: 1 + max(left, right). **O(n)** time.

title: Invert Binary Tree
description:
Swap every left/right child (mirror the tree). Input/output as level-order arrays.

**Example**
```
Input:  [4,2,7,1,3,6,9]
Output: [4,7,2,9,6,3,1]
```

Recursive swap. **O(n)** time.

title: Same Tree
description:
Check if two binary trees are structurally identical with same values.

**Example**
```
Input:  p=[1,2,3], q=[1,2,3]
Output: true
```

**O(n)** time. Recursive comparison.

title: Fibonacci Number
description:
Compute the nth Fibonacci number. F(0)=0, F(1)=1, F(n)=F(n-1)+F(n-2).

**Example**
```
Input:  n=4
Output: 3 (0,1,1,2,3)
```

Recursive with memoization to avoid O(2^n).

title: Power of Three
description:
Return true if integer is power of three.

**Example**
```
Input:  n=27
Output: true (3³)
```

Recursive: base case n≤0→false, n=1→true. Check n%3≠0→false, else recurse n/3.

title: Reverse String (Recursive)
description:
Reverse a string using recursion. No loops or .reverse().

**Example**
```
Input:  "hello"
Output: "olleh"
```

rev(s) = rev(s[1:]) + s[0]. Base: len ≤ 1.
